name: cupid-wpasupplicant
title: cupid-wpasupplicant
summary: Fork of wpasupplicant to exploit hertbleed vulnerability on wireless networks
description:
Fork of wpasupplicant to exploit hertbleed vulnerability on wireless networks
cupid-wpasupplicant provides a binary of the same name that has been patched
to exploit the heartbleed vulnerability over EAP TLS tunneled protocols
(EAP-PEAP, EAP-TLS, EAP-TTLS) in use in wireless networks.
.
With cupid-wpasupplicant you can try to exploit the vulnerability on
wireless access points.
.
Please see presentation slides for a simple introduction to cupid:
http://www.slideshare.net/lgrangeia

commands.name: cupid-wpa_passphrase
commands.description: Generate a WPA PSK from an ASCII passphrase for a SSID

arguments.description: The SSID whose passphrase should be derived.
arguments.raw: -bit  The SSID whose passphrase should be derived.

arguments.description: network configuration blocks of a wpa_suppli-
arguments.raw: -computes  network configuration blocks of a wpa_suppli-

commands.name: cupid-wpa_supplicant
commands.description: Wi-Fi Protected Access client and IEEE 802.1X supplicant

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cupid-wpa_passphrase because Fork of wpasupplicant to exploit hertbleed vulnerability on wireless networks.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cupid-wpa_passphrase as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cupid-wpa_passphrase with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cupid-wpa_passphrase --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -bit
arguments.description: The SSID whose passphrase should be derived.

arguments.name: -computes
arguments.description: network configuration blocks of a wpa_suppli-
